pattern: Walking through every number's decimal digits one at a time and tallying a running total whenever the target digit shows up directly produces the overall count.
hints: Convert each number in the array to its decimal digit sequence one at a time.
For each number, count how many of its own digits equal the target digit.
Add each number's individual count to a running total across the whole array.

edge_inputs.description: the target digit appears a different number of times in different elements
the target digit never appears anywhere
a single-element array with a single-digit value
the digit zero must be counted even though it never appears as a leading digit
a repdigit number where the target digit accounts for every digit position
many elements each contributing a single occurrence

stress.description: random array and target digit across the full bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    digit = rng.randint(0, 9)
    return [nums, digit]
